items.summary: Red Lobster has been around since 1968. Damola Adamolekun, its millennial chief executive, has already pulled the company out of bankruptcy.

items.headline: Brad Blum Knows How To Make Something From Nothing
items.summary: Brad Blum knows better than most that it's never what you say that makes a great leader.  Few actions in Blum's lifetime reflect that better than when, as a product manager for the Wheaties cereal brand at General Mills, he was touring a cereal production facility back in the mid-1980s, when a Black factory worker looked Blum in the eye and asked: Why hasn't a Black person been on a Wheaties package?  Blum had no answer.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Is It Time To Reconsider Darden Restaurants (DRI) After Mixed Recent Share Price Performance
items.summary: Wondering whether Darden Restaurants at around US$200.91 is offering value or asking you to pay up for quality? This article focuses squarely on what the current share price might mean for you. The stock is up 1.3% over the past week and 2.4% over the last month. Year to date it is up 7.3%, while the return over the last year is a decline of 4.2%, so recent moves sit against a mixed set of trailing returns. Over recent months, Darden Restaurants has stayed on investors' radar as a large US...

items.headline: 2 Restaurant Stocks to Research Further and 1 We Find Risky
items.summary: From fast food to fine dining, restaurants play a vital societal role. But the side dish is that they’re quite difficult to operate because high inventory and labor costs generally lead to thin margins at the store level. This leaves little room for error if demand dries up, and it seems like the market has some reservations as the industry has tumbled by 5.6% over the past six months. This drop is a noticeable divergence from the S&P 500’s 11% return.
items.why_it_matters: News may increase risk, valuation pressure, or negative sentiment.
